quadrangular: of or relating to or shaped like a quadrangle
quadruplex: quadruple rhythm has four beats per measure
quaggy: the ground was boggy under foot
quaint: strange in an interesting or pleasing way
very strange or unusual; odd or even incongruous in character or appearance
the head terminating in the quaint duck bill which gives the animal its vernacular name
qualified: meeting the proper standards and requirements and training for an office or position or task
many qualified applicants for the job
holding appropriate documentation and officially on record as qualified to perform a specified function or practice a specified skill
restricted in meaning; (as e.g. `man' in `a tall man')
qualitative: relating to or involving comparisons based on qualities
quantal: of or relating to a quantum or capable of existing in only one of two states
quantitative: expressible as a quantity or relating to or susceptible of measurement
relating to the measurement of quantity
(of verse) having a metric system based on relative duration of syllables
quantized: of or relating to a quantum or capable of existing in only one of two states
quarantined: under forced isolation especially for health reasons
quartzose: relating to or made of quartz
quasi-royal: having the power but not the rank or title of a king
one of the quasi-royal rulers of Africa
quaternate: consisting of or especially arranged in sets of four
quavering: (of the voice) quivering as from weakness or fear
queasy: causing or able to cause nausea
feeling nausea; feeling about to vomit
causing or fraught with or showing anxiety
spent an anxious night waiting for the test results
queen-size: (used especially of beds) not as large as king-size
queen-sized: (used especially of beds) not as large as king-size
queenlike: having the rank of or resembling or befitting a queen
queenly: having the rank of or resembling or befitting a queen
quenched: his thirst quenched he was able to continue
quick-change: adept at changing from one thing to another especially changing costumes
quick-drying: of a liquid substance that dries quickly
quick-frozen: (used of foods) preserved by freezing sufficiently rapidly to retain flavor and nutritional value
quick-sighted: quick-sighted into the faults of the time
quiescent: the quiescent level of centimeter wave-length solar radiation
marked by a state of tranquil repose
the quiescent melancholy of the town
being quiet or still or inactive
quilted: made of layers of fabric held together by patterned stitching
quinquefoliate: (of a leaf shape) having five leaflets
quintessential: representing the perfect example of a class or quality
quits: on equal terms by payment or requital
quixotic: not sensible about practical matters; idealistic and unrealistic
as quixotic as a restoration of medieval knighthood
quizzical: his face wore a somewhat quizzical almost impertinent air
perplexed (as if being expected to know something that you do not know)
quotable: able or fit to be repeated or quoted
what he said was not repeatable in polite company
suitable for or worthy of quotation
quotidian: found in the ordinary course of events
quaintly: in a strange but not unpleasant manner
the old lady expressed herself somewhat quaintly
qualitatively: this discoloration qualitatively suggests that the substance is low in inorganic iron
quaveringly: the man began quaveringly to question the soldier
queasily: `Do I have to remove the liver,' the medical student asked queasily
queerly: a queerly inscribed sheet of paper
this money had been queerly come by
questionably: in a questionable and dubious manner
these were estates his father questionably acquired
questioningly: in a curious and questioning manner
they turned their heads questioningly, as if awaiting further instructions
in a quizzical and questioning manner
they looked quizzically at the doctor
quickly: he looked cursorily through the magazine
quietly: speak softly but carry a big stick
the class was listening quietly and intently
with little or no activity or no agitation (`quiet' is a nonstandard variant for `quietly')
her hands rested quietly in her lap
the streets are restfully sunny and still for the town is at mass
quite: to a degree (not used with a negative)
of an unusually noticeable or exceptional or remarkable kind (not used with a negative)
actually or truly or to an extreme
quixotically: sent to jail for two years, he has quixotically refused to clear himself by betraying his colleagues
quizzically: in a quizzical and questioning manner
they looked quizzically at the doctor
